daily_brief: The US-Israeli war against Iran entered its second month with no clear diplomatic breakthrough, as Secretary of State Marco Rubio told G7 counterparts that Washington expects to conclude operations within weeks [France 24, RFI]. The conflict widened significantly with Yemen's Houthi rebels launching their first missile toward Israel since the war began [Le Monde, The Hindu], while an Iranian strike on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia wounded 12 US troops and damaged aircraft [Globe and Mail, Financial Times]. G7 foreign ministers issued a joint statement calling for an immediate end to attacks on civilians and urging Iran to restore free navigation through the Strait of Hormuz [RFI]. On the domestic front, the US faces mounting chaos as House Republicans rejected a Senate deal to fund the Department of Homeland Security, leaving TSA agents unpaid for over a month and causing widespread airport delays; President Trump signed an executive order to pay TSA workers as a stopgap [BBC, Globe and Mail]. In a separate development, the UN General Assembly passed a landmark resolution declaring the transatlantic slave trade 'the gravest crime against humanity,' with Western nations including the UK and EU abstaining [The Guardian, BBC]. Nepal's former Prime Minister KP Sharma Oli was arrested over his role in a deadly protest crackdown that killed over 70 people last year [BBC].

top_stories.headline: US-Israeli War on Iran Enters Second Month as Houthis Fire First Missile at Israel
bullets: The Israeli military reported the first missile launch from Yemen toward Israeli territory since the war began on February 28, with aerial defense systems operating to intercept the threat
Yemen's Houthi movement warned it would join the conflict if US-Israeli attacks continue against Iran or if other nations enter the war
Secretary of State Marco Rubio stated the US expects to conclude military operations within weeks, not months, without deploying ground troops
The war has killed more than 1,937 people in Iran and displaced approximately one quarter of Lebanon's population

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Focused on military developments and the Houthi warning

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized US confidence in quick resolution and Lebanese PM interview

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Highlighted civilian suffering in Lebanon and Iran, more critical of US-Israeli campaign

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Neutral reporting on military developments and regional implications

sources.name: RFI
sources.framing_note: Focused on G7 diplomatic response and call for civilian protection

top_stories.headline: Iranian Strike Wounds 12 US Troops at Saudi Air Base, Damages Aircraft
bullets: An Iranian attack on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia wounded 12 US service members, two of them seriously
Several aerial refueling planes were damaged in the strike, which involved at least one missile and several drones
The casualties bring the total US wounded since February 28 to over 300, with 13 US troops killed in the conflict
Iran agreed to allow humanitarian aid and agricultural shipments through the Strait of Hormuz following a UN request

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Led with the attack but also highlighted the humanitarian corridor breakthrough

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Brief coverage linking to Houthi developments

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Straightforward casualty reporting with regional context

top_stories.headline: US Airport Chaos Deepens as House Rejects DHS Funding Deal, Trump Signs Emergency Pay Order
bullets: House Republicans rejected a Senate deal to fund the Transportation Security Administration, extending the DHS partial shutdown to 44 days
TSA agents have not been paid for over a month, causing widespread delays at major US airports
President Trump signed an executive order directing payment to TSA employees, citing an emergency compromising national security
The order may face legal challenges as the Constitution assigns spending authority to Congress

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized political impasse and constitutional concerns

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Focused on Trump's executive action and practical implications for travelers

top_stories.headline: UN General Assembly Declares Transatlantic Slave Trade 'Gravest Crime Against Humanity'
bullets: The UN General Assembly passed a resolution by 123 votes to 3, with 52 abstentions, declaring the transatlantic slave trade the gravest crime against humanity
The US, Argentina, and Israel voted against; the UK and EU member states abstained
Ghana's President John Mahama led the push for the resolution, which is not legally binding but strengthens momentum for reparations discussions
UN Secretary-General Guterres said Western nations' wealth was 'built on stolen lives and stolen labour'

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Extensive analysis of voting patterns and reparations implications, highlighted African leadership

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Balanced explanation of the resolution's meaning and personal impact on descendants

top_stories.headline: Nepal's Former PM Oli Arrested Over Deadly Protest Crackdown
bullets: Former Prime Minister KP Sharma Oli and ex-Home Minister Ramesh Lekhak were arrested following recommendations from an investigative panel
More than 70 people were killed during protests in September 2025, many shot by police during an uprising sparked by a social media ban
The arrests came one day after 35-year-old rapper-turned-politician Balen Shah was sworn in as new Prime Minister
Oli rejected the commission's findings as 'character assassination' and his lawyers said the detention was unwarranted

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Comprehensive coverage including Oli's defense and context of political transition

additional_stories.headline: Tiger Woods arrested on DUI charges after rollover crash in Florida; authorities suspect drug impairment
EU reaches agreement to regulate flood of small parcels from Chinese e-commerce platforms Shein, Temu, and AliExpress
Pope Leo XIV makes first papal visit to Monaco in nearly 500 years
Bank of America agrees to pay $72.5 million to settle class-action lawsuit alleging facilitation of Epstein sex-trafficking ring
Trump threatens Cuba with military action, saying 'Cuba is next' at Miami investment conference
Two drone strikes in Sudan kill 28 civilians, including an infant, as civil war approaches fourth year
Egypt imposes 9pm business curfew to counter energy costs that have more than doubled due to Iran war
Pakistan and IMF reach staff-level agreement for disbursement of approximately $1.2 billion in funding
Austria announces plans to ban social media for children under 14, joining growing international movement
Zelenskyy says US has linked security guarantees in peace deal to Ukraine ceding all of Donbas to Russia
